tweet: shipped a redesign. we now have a proper palette system, new scene backgrounds, and updated colors across the board. turns out having an actual color system is easier than guessing hex codes every time.
alt_tweet: pushed some fresh paint to pollinations.ai today. new scene backgrounds, updated colors, and a unified palette system. less chaos, more lime green.
image_prompt: Dynamic pixel art scene of a cute pixel bee mascot wearing a tiny painter's beret, holding a large pixelated wooden palette and a paintbrush. The bee has a yellow body, dark brown horizontal stripes, small black legs, black antennae, black vertical oval eyes, bright pink rectangular blush marks, a small happy smile, and light blue translucent wings. She is actively painting a bright lime green (#ecf874) swoosh across a soft pastel lavender and peach background. A chunky retro robot with a CRT monitor head stands nearby holding a bucket of fresh pixel paint. High contrast within the brand palette, energetic scroll-stopping composition. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi retro gaming vibes like Stardew Valley.
reasoning: Focuses on the concrete update (palette system) while using dry humor about developer habits (guessing hex codes).
